title: georgina rosea
intro: Georgina rosea, also known as Rosa rubiginosa, is a species of rose native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its fragrant pink flowers and edible hips. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its hips, which contain high amounts of vitamin C, as well as other antioxidants and flavonoids. The health benefits of Georgina rosea include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Georgina rosea can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
